hypothesis: If a single score-based diffusion model is trained only on metastable-state data and its reverse SDE is guided by interpolated conditional scores and then refined with score-aligned ascent, then the generated configurations will concentrate near true transition states (committor ≈ 0.5) and outperform linear/geodesic interpolation baselines across diverse systems because the difference of conditional scores approximates the reaction coordinate and force projection focuses sampling onto the barrier.
